substep.title: Persist Threads to Assessment Workspace
substep.action:
Write the raw `unresolved_threads` array from `await_pr_review` to: {{bundle_dir}}/pr-cycle-threads-iter-{{iteration_count}}.json
This is the input the validator subagent will assess against.

invoke-agent.name: codesmith-validator
invoke-agent.prompt:
Assess PR #{{pr_number}} review threads against the actual codebase.
Inputs: - Threads JSON: {{bundle_dir}}/pr-cycle-threads-iter-{{iteration_count}}.json - Branch: {{branch}} - Worktree: {{worktree_path}} - Bundle: {{bundle_dir}}
For EACH thread: 1. Spawn codebase-analyzer to read the file:line in context. 2. Understand WHY the code was written this way (cross-reference the bundle's design.md and plan-*.md). 3. Evaluate Gemini's concern against codebase reality + design intent. 4. Recommend: Address | Decline | Defer - Address: Gemini is correct; the code needs a change. Specify the fix in concrete terms. - Decline: Gemini is wrong (false positive, missing context, or proposes a change that contradicts design). State the rationale. - Defer: Gemini is correct but the fix exceeds this PR's scope. State why deferral is appropriate.
Output: JSON to stdout matching the assessment schema. Persist a copy at {{bundle_dir}}/pr-cycle-assessment-iter-{{iteration_count}}.json.
DO NOT rubber-stamp. Every thread requires evidence-bound assessment.
